files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding domain param support to trans tag: will edit InternationalizationExtension.parse() (parse domain="string" param, validate string literal, error on duplicate), _make_node() (use dgettext/dngettext when domain given), and _install_callables() (install dgettext/dngettext callables into environment globals).

type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding trans_metadata support: editing InternationalizationExtension.parse() while-loop (parameter parsing) and _make_node() method signature/body in src/jinja2/ext.py

type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Need detail on trans_metadata: what syntax/keyword does it use in the trans tag parameters (e.g. metadata="..."), and does it change the loop's token.value branching (assign / trimmed keyword) or add a new keyword before variables? Want to know exact param name and how it's stored/passed to _make_node signature so we can merge both changes into one loop and one _make_node call.

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension.parse() method
code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at
        # a later state.
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            # expressions
            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)

                if token.value in _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS:
                    value_node = parser.parse_expression()
                    if not (
                        isinstance(value_node, nodes.Const)
                        and isinstance(value_node.value, str)
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a"
                            " string literal.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    metadata[token.value] = value_node.value
                    continue

                if token.value == "domain":
                    if domain is not None:
                        parser.fail(
                            "translation domain defined twice.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    value_node = parser.parse_expression()
                    if not (
                        isinstance(value_node, nodes.Const)
                        and isinstance(value_node.value, str)
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            "domain parameter must be a string literal.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    domain = value_node.value
                    continue

                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            elif token.value in _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS:
                parser.fail(
                    f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value"
                    " assignment.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:
            referenced.update(singular_names)
            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
            have_plural = True
            next(parser.stream)
            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
                if token.value not in variables:
                    parser.fail(
                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
            next(parser.stream)
            referenced.update(plural_names)
        else:
            next(parser.stream)

        # register free names as simple name expressions
        for name in referenced:
            if name not in variables:
                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")

        if not have_plural:
            plural_expr = None
        elif plural_expr is None:
            parser.fail("pluralize without variables", lineno)

        if trimmed is None:
            trimmed = self.environment.policies["ext.i18n.trimmed"]
        if trimmed:
            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
            if plural:
                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)

        node = self._make_node(
            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,
            domain,
            metadata,
        )
        node.set_lineno(lineno)
        if plural_expr_assignment is not None:
            return [plural_expr_assignment, node]
        else:
            return node
note: Merged: my metadata (note/created/modified) string-literal parsing + your domain string-literal parsing, both as continue-branches inside the assign check; introduces module-level _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS = ('note','created','modified') constant. _make_node call now passes domain and metadata as new trailing args. Please review/adjust the domain bits to match your intended design, then send back a DRAFT if anything differs.

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node() method
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_prefix = "d" if domain is not None else ""
        func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = (
            [nodes.Const(domain)] if domain is not None else []
        )

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            gettext = nodes.Name(f"{func_prefix}gettext", "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, [*func_args, nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            ngettext = nodes.Name(f"{func_prefix}ngettext", "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                [*func_args, nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )

        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output_node
note: Merged: domain param switches gettext/ngettext to dgettext/dngettext with domain as first Const arg (assumes you install dgettext/dngettext globals in _install_callables separately - that part is yours, not touched here). metadata param sets output_node.trans_metadata only when non-empty. Let me know if dgettext/dngettext call signature should differ.

type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: answer to your SURVEY: trans_metadata syntax
note: Syntax: {% trans note="...", created="...", modified="..." %} - three reserved param names (note, created, modified), each requires assign to a string literal (parser.parse_expression() result must be nodes.Const with str value). They branch inside the existing 'if parser.stream.current.type == "assign"' block via membership in a new module-level tuple _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS = ('note','created','modified'), each match does 'continue' (does NOT go into variables dict). If one of these names appears WITHOUT assign, it's an error (metadata parameter requires a value assignment) added as a new elif branch alongside the existing trimmed/notrimmed elif. Collected into local dict metadata: t.Dict[str,str] = {} initialized before the while loop, passed as a new trailing arg to _make_node(). Full merged parse() and _make_node() code already sent in my two prior DRAFT messages - please diff against those and reply with AGREE or a new DRAFT if you want changes.

code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno
        num_called_num = False

        # find all the variables referenced.  Additionally a variable can be
        # defined in the body of the trans block too, but this is checked at

        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None
        plural_expr_assignment: t.Optional[nodes.Assign] = None
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr] = {}
        trimmed = None
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None
        metadata: t.Dict[str, str] = {}
        while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")

            # skip colon for python compatibility
            if parser.stream.skip_if("colon"):
                break

            token = parser.stream.expect("name")
            if token.value in variables:
                parser.fail(
                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )

            # expressions
            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)

                if token.value in _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS:
                    value_node = parser.parse_expression()
                    if not (
                        isinstance(value_node, nodes.Const)
                        and isinstance(value_node.value, str)
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} must be a"
                            " string literal.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    metadata[token.value] = value_node.value
                    continue

                if token.value == "domain":
                    if domain is not None:
                        parser.fail(
                            "translation domain defined twice.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    value_node = parser.parse_expression()
                    if not (
                        isinstance(value_node, nodes.Const)
                        and isinstance(value_node.value, str)
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            "domain parameter must be a string literal.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    domain = value_node.value
                    continue

                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue
            elif token.value in _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS:
                parser.fail(
                    f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value"
                    " assignment.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                )
            else:
                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var
                    )
                else:
                    plural_expr = var
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

        parser.stream.expect("block_end")

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        # now parse until endtrans or pluralize
        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:
            referenced.update(singular_names)
            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too
        if parser.stream.current.test("name:pluralize"):
            have_plural = True
            next(parser.stream)
            if parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
                token = parser.stream.expect("name")
                if token.value not in variables:
                    parser.fail(
                        f"unknown variable {token.value!r} for pluralization",
                        token.lineno,
                        exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                    )
                plural_expr = variables[token.value]
                num_called_num = token.value == "num"
            parser.stream.expect("block_end")
            plural_names, plural = self._parse_block(parser, False)
            next(parser.stream)
            referenced.update(plural_names)
        else:
            next(parser.stream)

        # register free names as simple name expressions
        for name in referenced:
            if name not in variables:
                variables[name] = nodes.Name(name, "load")

        if not have_plural:
            plural_expr = None
        elif plural_expr is None:
            parser.fail("pluralize without variables", lineno)

        if trimmed:
            singular = self._trim_whitespace(singular)
            if plural:
                plural = self._trim_whitespace(plural)

            singular,
            plural,
            variables,
            plural_expr,
            bool(referenced),
            num_called_num and have_plural,

        )

            return [plural_expr_assignment, node]

code:     def parse(self, parser: "Parser") -> t.Union[nodes.Node, t.List[nodes.Node]]:
        """Parse a translatable tag."""
        lineno = next(parser.stream).lineno

                break

                    f"translatable variable {token.value!r} defined twice.",

                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,

            if parser.stream.current.type == "assign":
                next(parser.stream)

                if token.value in _TRANS_METADATA_KEYS:
                    value_node = parser.parse_expression()

                        isinstance(value_node, nodes.Const)
                        and isinstance(value_node.value, str)

                            " string literal.",

                    continue

                    if domain is not None:

                            "translation domain defined twice.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    value_node = parser.parse_expression()
                    if not (
                        isinstance(value_node, nodes.Const)
                        and isinstance(value_node.value, str)
                    ):
                        parser.fail(
                            "domain parameter must be a string literal.",
                            token.lineno,
                            exc=TemplateAssertionError,
                        )
                    domain = value_node.value
                    continue

                variables[token.value] = var = parser.parse_expression()
            elif trimmed is None and token.value in ("trimmed", "notrimmed"):
                trimmed = token.value == "trimmed"
                continue

                parser.fail(
                    f"metadata parameter {token.value!r} requires a value"
                    " assignment.",
                    token.lineno,
                    exc=TemplateAssertionError,

                variables[token.value] = var = nodes.Name(token.value, "load")

            if plural_expr is None:
                if isinstance(var, nodes.Call):
                    plural_expr = nodes.Name("_trans", "load")
                    variables[token.value] = plural_expr
                    plural_expr_assignment = nodes.Assign(
                        nodes.Name("_trans", "store"), var

                else:
                    plural_expr = var

        plural = None
        have_plural = False
        referenced = set()

        singular_names, singular = self._parse_block(parser, True)
        if singular_names:

            if plural_expr is None:
                plural_expr = nodes.Name(singular_names[0], "load")
                num_called_num = singular_names[0] == "num"

        # if we have a pluralize block, we parse that too

            next(parser.stream)

                num_called_num = token.value == "num"

files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node() method
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            func_name = "dgettext" if domain is not None else "gettext"
            func = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            args = [nodes.Const(singular)]
            if domain is not None:
                args.insert(0, nodes.Const(domain))
            node = nodes.Call(func, args, [], None, None)

        # singular and plural
        else:
            func_name = "dngettext" if domain is not None else "ngettext"
            func = nodes.Name(func_name, "load")
            args = [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr]
            if domain is not None:
                args.insert(0, nodes.Const(domain))
            node = nodes.Call(func, args, [], None, None)

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )
        return nodes.Output([node])
note: domain: prepends domain const arg and switches gettext/ngettext to dgettext/dngettext. metadata param accepted here for signature-compat with your parse() call, but I did not add any node-generation logic for it since I don't know if you need it to affect the emitted node (vs. just being available for extraction elsewhere). If you need to consume metadata inside this method, propose the exact insertion (e.g. attaching as a comment/attribute) and I'll fold it in verbatim before we both implement.

                    ),
                )

        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output_node

files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: InternationalizationExtension._make_node() method
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[str] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        func_prefix = "d" if domain is not None else ""
        func_args: t.List[nodes.Expr] = (
            [nodes.Const(domain)] if domain is not None else []
        )

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            gettext = nodes.Name(f"{func_prefix}gettext", "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                gettext, [*func_args, nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
            )

        # singular and plural
        else:
            ngettext = nodes.Name(f"{func_prefix}ngettext", "load")
            node = nodes.Call(
                ngettext,
                [*func_args, nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                [],
                None,
                None,
            )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )

        output_node = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output_node.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output_node

region: parse() comma-consumption fix (bugfix to previously-agreed code)
code:         while parser.stream.current.type != "block_end":
            if variables:
                parser.stream.expect("comma")
            else:

note: Found a bug while testing our agreed parse(): the comma-consumption check 'if variables: parser.stream.expect("comma")' only fires once a real variable has been added to . Since metadata/domain params never touch , sequences like {% trans note="a", created="b" %} (both metadata, no real variable) leave the comma unconsumed -> TemplateSyntaxError 'expected name, got ,'. Required per spec: '{% trans note="User count", created="2024-01-01" %}...'. Fix: add an else-branch that optionally skips a comma when variables is still empty (mirrors the pre-existing lenient behavior used by trimmed/notrimmed, e.g. test_trimmed_vars which omits the comma entirely). Verified: all 47 existing tests in tests/test_ext.py still pass, plus all my new metadata scenarios. Please apply the identical 5-line change (adds an else: parser.stream.skip_if("comma") branch) to your copy of parse() and AGREE.
